task_completion.description: Open the master model in OpenVSP
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenVSP application window is open, displaying the 3D geometry of the eCRM-001 wing and tail.
A text editor (like gedit, nano) or terminal (cat/less) displaying the contents of 'structural_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Open the Structure properties panel for the main Wing
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP Geometry browser shows the main 'Wing' component selected, and the 'Structure' or 'Internal Structure' properties window/tab is open.

task_completion.description: Add a Front Spar at 22% chord
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Structure panel, a spar is listed and its location (chord fraction) is set to 0.22 (or 22%).

task_completion.description: Add a Rear Spar at 68% chord
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Structure panel, a second spar is listed and its location (chord fraction) is set to 0.68 (or 68%).

task_completion.description: Configure the wing to have exactly 34 ribs
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Structure panel under the Ribs section, the number of ribs (Count) is set to 34.

task_completion.description: Save the updated model to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in OpenVSP showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/eCRM001_structural.vsp3', or terminal/file manager evidence that this specific file was created.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenVSP GUI to modify the structure
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenVSP Structure panel with the spar and rib values entered manually, rather than a terminal window running a script or text editor modifying the .vsp3 file directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the correct eCRM-001 model
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport in OpenVSP must show the complex eCRM-001 wing and tail geometry, not a default or primitive shape.

integrity.description: Agent saved the file using OpenVSP
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP 'File' menu or 'Save Vehicle As' dialog should be visible, indicating the software itself is writing the file, rather than a simple terminal copy/rename command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/eCRM001_structural.vsp3'. The structural specifications to look for in the OpenVSP Structure panel are: Front Spar at 0.22 chord, Rear Spar at 0.68 chord, and exactly 34 ribs. A tolerance of 0.01 is acceptable for the spar locations.
